cmd/jobs/entitlement.purpose: Cobra sub-command group for entitlement administrative jobs; currently owns one job (recalculate-balance-snapshots) that recalculates metered entitlement balance snapshots and publishes results to the event bus via balanceworker.Recalculator.

patterns.name: Access app state via internal.App global
patterns.description: All job commands read dependencies (EntitlementRegistry, EventPublisher, NotificationService, Customer, Subject, Meter, Logger) from the package-level internal.App struct, never constructing services themselves.

patterns.name: Root + sub-command structure
patterns.description: root.go defines RootCommand() returning a parent cobra.Command with Use='entitlement', and each job file adds a sub-command via cmd.AddCommand in RootCommand.

patterns.name: Use cmd.Context() for context propagation
patterns.description: RunE closures pass cmd.Context() to domain calls, never context.Background().

key_file_guides.role: Defines the 'entitlement' parent Cobra command and registers all sub-commands.
key_file_guides.watch_for: New jobs must be added via cmd.AddCommand here to be reachable.

key_file_guides.role: Implements recalculate-balance-snapshots job using balanceworker.NewRecalculator; canonical example of job structure.
key_file_guides.watch_for: All RecalculatorOptions fields come from internal.App — adding a dependency requires updating internal/wire.go Application struct first.

anti_patterns: Constructing domain services (e.g. balanceworker.Recalculator dependencies) inside the command instead of sourcing from internal.App
Adding business logic to RunE beyond orchestrating internal.App services

decisions.decision: Dependencies come exclusively from internal.App global rather than local construction.
decisions.rationale: Wire-generated initializeApplication in cmd/jobs/internal produces a fully wired Application; repeating wiring in job commands would duplicate provider graphs and risk inconsistency.

code_examples.scenario: Adding a new entitlement job sub-command

cmd/jobs/internal.purpose: Provides the Wire-wired Application struct, config loading, and package-level globals (App, AppShutdown, Config) shared by all cmd/jobs sub-commands; it is the single DI entry point for the jobs binary.

patterns.name: Wire-generated initializeApplication
patterns.description: wire.go declares the Application struct and calls wire.Build with common.* provider sets; wire_gen.go is the generated output. Never edit wire_gen.go manually.

patterns.name: Application struct as the service registry
patterns.description: Every domain service needed by any job sub-command is a field on Application. Adding a dependency requires adding it as a field here and regenerating wire_gen.go with 'make generate'.
patterns.example: type Application struct { common.GlobalInitializer; BillingRegistry common.BillingRegistry; EntitlementRegistry *registry.Entitlement; ... }

patterns.name: Two-phase startup: loadConfig then initializeApplication
patterns.description: app.go calls loadConfig first to populate Config, then calls Wire-generated initializeApplication(ctx, Config) to build App. Sub-commands call internal.InitializeApplication from their PersistentPreRunE.

patterns.name: Package-level globals with nolint:gochecknoglobals
patterns.description: App, AppShutdown, Config, ConfigFile are deliberately package-level globals annotated with //nolint:gochecknoglobals — do not change to local variables.
patterns.example: var App Application; var AppShutdown func(); var Config config.Configuration

patterns.name: metadata function maps config to common.Metadata
patterns.description: The metadata() function in wire.go bridges config.Configuration to common.Metadata using the service name 'jobs'; this is how all binaries stamp their telemetry identity.
patterns.example: func metadata(conf config.Configuration) common.Metadata { return common.NewMetadata(conf, version, "jobs") }

key_file_guides.role: Declares Application struct and wire.Build injection; source of truth for which common.* provider sets are included for jobs binary.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Adding a new domain service to Application requires adding the corresponding common.* provider set in wire.Build, then running 'make generate'.

key_file_guides.role: Generated by Wire — DO NOT EDIT. Shows the exact construction order and cleanup chain.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Never hand-edit; re-run 'make generate' after any wire.go change.

key_file_guides.role: Defines the four package-level globals consumed by all job sub-packages.
key_file_guides.watch_for: All sub-command packages read from internal.App — fields must exist before a sub-command can reference them.

key_file_guides.role: Public entrypoint InitializeApplication called by cmd/jobs/main.go's PersistentPreRunE.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Order matters: loadConfig before initializeApplication; any config validation failure aborts before Wire wiring runs.

key_file_guides.role: Loads and validates app/config.Configuration using Viper; uses config.DecodeHook() for custom type unmarshal.
key_file_guides.watch_for: ConfigFileNotFoundError is tolerated (env-var only config); other read errors are fatal.

anti_patterns: Editing wire_gen.go directly instead of modifying wire.go and regenerating
Adding domain service construction logic inside app.go or config.go instead of wire.go provider sets
Importing cmd/jobs/internal from app/common (would create import cycle)
Adding a new sub-command field to Application without a corresponding common.* provider in wire.Build

decisions.decision: All domain wiring is delegated to app/common provider sets rather than inline construction.
decisions.rationale: Centralizing providers in app/common means all seven binaries share the same wiring logic; changes to a constructor signature surface at Wire generation time, not at runtime.

decisions.decision: Package-level globals for App/Config rather than passing via cobra context.
decisions.rationale: Cobra's PersistentPreRunE runs before any sub-command RunE, making globals the simplest cross-package handoff without threading context through every layer.

code_examples.scenario: Adding a new service (e.g. CurrenciesService) to the jobs Application

cmd/jobs/llmcost.purpose: Cobra sub-command group for LLM cost database operations; exposes a single 'sync' command that delegates to the Wire-provided LLMCostSyncJob from internal.App.

patterns.name: Package-level Cmd var with init() sub-command registration
patterns.description: Cmd is declared as a package-level *cobra.Command; sub-commands are registered in init() via Cmd.AddCommand, unlike the entitlement package which uses RootCommand(). Both styles exist — match the file you're extending.

patterns.name: Delegate to internal.App service, never construct
patterns.description: RunE calls internal.App.LLMCostSyncJob.Run(cmd.Context()) directly; no local service construction.

key_file_guides.role: Defines the 'llm-cost' parent command and the 'sync' sub-command; canonical example of the init()-based sub-command registration pattern.
key_file_guides.watch_for: LLMCostSyncJob must exist on internal.App (it's wired via common.LLMCost in wire.go); removing it from Application would break this command at compile time.

anti_patterns: Constructing llmcost.SyncJob directly instead of using internal.App.LLMCostSyncJob

cmd/jobs/migrate.purpose: Cobra sub-command for running database migrations via the Wire-provided Migrator; validates the requested migration mode before executing, and refuses the 'job' mode to avoid self-referential migration loops.

patterns.name: Mutate internal.App.Migrator.Config before calling Migrate
patterns.description: The migration mode flag overrides App.Migrator.Config.AutoMigrate at runtime rather than wiring a separate migrator. This is the only place in jobs that mutates App state.

patterns.name: Validate mode before execution
patterns.description: Uses config.AutoMigrate() to parse and validate the mode string; explicitly rejects AutoMigrateMigrationJob mode to prevent circular use.
patterns.example: if !migrationMode.Enabled() { return fmt.Errorf("migration mode is disabled") }

key_file_guides.role: Implements the 'migrate' Cobra command; wraps common.Migrator with mode validation. Default mode is 'migration' (config.AutoMigrateMigration).
key_file_guides.watch_for: The --mode flag accepts only 'ent' and 'migration'; 'job' mode is explicitly rejected. Do not add business logic beyond mode selection and Migrate() invocation.

anti_patterns: Calling tools/migrate directly instead of going through internal.App.Migrator
Adding migration logic (DDL, data transforms) to this command — those belong in tools/migrate/migrations/

decisions.decision: Migration mode is overridden on App.Migrator.Config rather than constructing a new migrator.
decisions.rationale: The Wire-provided Migrator already has the correct DB client and logger; overriding just the mode reuses all that wiring without duplication.

cmd/jobs/quickstart.purpose: Cobra sub-command group for docker-compose quickstart helpers; provides a 'cron' command that schedules billing and subscription sync loops using gocron, intended only for quickstart/demo environments.

patterns.name: gocron-based polling loops for billing lifecycle
patterns.description: cronjobs.go uses gocron.NewScheduler to schedule BillingSubscriptionReconciler.All, BillingCollector.All, BillingAutoAdvancer.All, and (conditionally) ChargesAutoAdvancer.All at fixed intervals. Context cancellation shuts down the scheduler.

patterns.name: Optional feature guard via nil check
patterns.description: ChargesAutoAdvancer may be nil when charges are disabled; code guards with if internal.App.ChargesAutoAdvancer != nil before scheduling.

patterns.name: Block on context cancellation, then shutdown scheduler
patterns.description: The cron command blocks on <-cmd.Context().Done() and calls s.Shutdown() before returning, ensuring graceful stop.
patterns.example: <-cmd.Context().Done(); if err := s.Shutdown(); err != nil { return err }

patterns.name: Composition of other job packages
patterns.description: quickstart.go adds sub-commands from other job packages (e.g. billing/subscriptionsync) via init() — it acts as a meta-group aggregating related operational commands.

key_file_guides.role: Defines the 'quickstart' parent command and composes sub-commands from other job packages.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Explicitly documented as not for production; do not add production-critical logic here.

key_file_guides.role: Implements 'cron' sub-command with gocron scheduler for billing lifecycle automation in quickstart environments.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Hardcodes namespace 'default' and small batchSize=10 — not suitable for multi-tenant production use. Any new cron job must null-guard optional services from internal.App before scheduling.

anti_patterns: Using this package's cron patterns in production workers (billing-worker already handles these via Kafka/Watermill)
Hardcoding namespaces inside quickstart cron tasks for production deployments
Not null-checking optional internal.App services (e.g. ChargesAutoAdvancer) before scheduling

decisions.decision: Quickstart cron uses gocron instead of Watermill/Kafka for simplicity.
decisions.rationale: The quickstart docker-compose environment may not have a full Kafka topology; gocron provides polling-based advancement without Kafka dependencies.
